sourceMovie.title: Just Like Heaven
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Just Like Heaven works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Swiss Army Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Swiss Army Man (2016) clearly understood what makes comedy work — the same quality that keeps Just Like Heaven rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Purple Rose of Cairo
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Purple Rose of Cairo (1985) is the kind of film Just Like Heaven fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The comedy overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Heart and Souls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Heart and Souls shares Just Like Heaven's gift for comedy — the 1993 film delivers romance without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Age of Adaline
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Just Like Heaven compelling — the romance, the fantasy — shows up in The Age of Adaline (2015) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that bittersweet storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: What Women Want
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The comedy energy of Just Like Heaven is alive in What Women Want (2000), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Sideways
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Sideways (2004) operates in the same comedy as Just Like Heaven, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Emma
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Emma (1996) belongs to the same conversation as Just Like Heaven — both anchor their comedy in romance emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Love Bug
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Just Like Heaven's comedy stayed with you, The Love Bug (1968) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The fantasy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Vertigo
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Just Like Heaven excels in romance, Vertigo (1958) doubles down on mystery to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Vow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Vow (2012) is the natural next step after Just Like Heaven: same romance DNA, different perspective. The bittersweet streak and drama elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

faq.question: What should I watch after Just Like Heaven?
faq.answer: Try Swiss Army Man, The Purple Rose of Cairo, Heart and Souls—each shares DNA with Just Like Heaven while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Just Like Heaven?
faq.answer: Just Like Heaven (2005) is best described as Comedy, Romance, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Just Like Heaven worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
